title: 2 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-2-1-1 Understanding arithmetic sequences and relationships, representing them graphically, distinguishing linear relationships, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: Tommy has 5 pencils in his pencil case. He adds 2 more pencils every day. What is the total number of pencils in his case after 3 days?
questions.explanation: The sequence of the total number of pencils in Tommy's case is 5, 7, 9, 11, ... . This sequence can be represented as a linear function y = 2x + 3, where x is the number of days and y is the total number of pencils.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 8 books on it. Every day, 2 more books are added to the bookshelf. How many books are on the bookshelf after 5 days?
questions.explanation: The sequence of the total number of books on the bookshelf is 8, 10, 12, 14, 16, ... . This sequence can be represented as a linear function y = 2x + 6, where x is the number of days and y is the total number of books.

questions.question: A toy car track is 12 meters long. Every day, 3 more meters of track are added. How long will the track be after 4 days?
questions.explanation: The sequence of the length of the track is 12, 15, 18, 21, ... . This sequence can be represented as a linear function y = 3x + 12, where x is the number of days and y is the length of the track.

questions.question: A farmer has 15 apples in his basket. Every day, 4 more apples are added to the basket. How many apples will be in the basket after 3 days?
questions.explanation: The sequence of the total number of apples in the basket is 15, 19, 23, ... . This sequence can be represented as a linear function y = 4x + 11, where x is the number of days and y is the total number of apples.

questions.question: A library has 20 books on a shelf. Every day, 5 more books are added to the shelf. How many books will be on the shelf after 2 days?
questions.explanation: The sequence of the total number of books on the shelf is 20, 25, ... . This sequence can be represented as a linear function y = 5x + 20, where x is the number of days and y is the total number of books.

questions.question: A water tank can hold 18 gallons of water. Every day, 3 more gallons of water are added to the tank. How many gallons of water will be in the tank after 6 days?
questions.explanation: The sequence of the total number of gallons of water in the tank is 18, 21, 24, 27, 30, ... . This sequence can be represented as a linear function y = 3x + 18, where x is the number of days and y is the total number of gallons.

questions.question: A toy factory produces 12 toys every hour. Every hour, 2 more toys are produced. How many toys will be produced after 5 hours?
questions.explanation: The sequence of the total number of toys produced is 12, 14, 16, 18, 20, ... . This sequence can be represented as a linear function y = 2x + 12, where x is the number of hours and y is the total number of toys.

questions.question: A farmer has 12 eggs in his carton. Every day, 4 more eggs are added to the carton. How many eggs will be in the carton after 4 days?
questions.explanation: The sequence of the total number of eggs in the carton is 12, 16, 20, 24, ... . This sequence can be represented as a linear function y = 4x + 12, where x is the number of days and y is the total number of eggs.

questions.question: A library has 15 books on a shelf. Every day, 3 more books are added to the shelf. How many books will be on the shelf after 2 days?
questions.explanation: The sequence of the total number of books on the shelf is 15, 18, ... . This sequence can be represented as a linear function y = 3x + 15, where x is the number of days and y is the total number of books.

questions.question: A water tank can hold 20 gallons of water. Every day, 4 more gallons of water are added to the tank. How many gallons of water will be in the tank after 5 days?
questions.explanation: The sequence of the total number of gallons of water in the tank is 20, 24, 28, 32, 36, ... . This sequence can be represented as a linear function y = 4x + 20, where x is the number of days and y is the total number of gallons.

title: 3 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-2-1-1 Understanding arithmetic sequences and relationships, representing them graphically, distinguishing linear relationships, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: Tom has 5 pencils in his pencil case. He adds 2 more pencils every day. How many pencils will he have in 3 days?
questions.explanation: Tom starts with 5 pencils and adds 2 more pencils each day. So, 5 + 2*2 = 9 pencils in 3 days.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 8 books on it. Every day, 2 more books are added. How many books will be on the bookshelf in 4 days?
questions.explanation: The bookshelf starts with 8 books and 2 more books are added each day. So, 8 + 2*4 = 16, but answer is 12 since the question is asking for the amount of books in 4 days.

questions.question: A toy car track is 10 meters long. Every day, 2 more meters of track are added. How many meters of track will be added in 5 days?
questions.explanation: The track starts at 10 meters and 2 more meters are added each day. So, 2*5 = 10 meters.

questions.question: A farmer has 12 apples. Every day, 4 more apples are picked. How many apples will the farmer have in 3 days?
questions.explanation: The farmer starts with 12 apples and 4 more apples are picked each day. So, 12 + 4*3 = 24, but answer is 16 since the question is asking for the amount of apples the farmer will have, not the amount of apples picked.

questions.question: A water tank can hold 20 gallons of water. Every day, 5 more gallons of water are added. How many gallons of water will be in the tank in 4 days?
questions.explanation: The tank starts at 20 gallons and 5 more gallons are added each day. So, 20 + 5*4 = 40, but answer is 35 since the question is asking for the amount of water in the tank, not the amount of water added.

questions.question: A student has 15 stickers. Every day, 3 more stickers are given to the student. How many stickers will the student have in 5 days?
questions.explanation: The student starts with 15 stickers and 3 more stickers are given each day. So, 15 + 3*5 = 30, but answer is 20 since the question is asking for the amount of stickers the student will have, not the amount of stickers given.

questions.question: A store has 12 boxes of toys. Every day, 2 more boxes are added. How many boxes of toys will be in the store in 6 days?
questions.explanation: The store starts at 12 boxes and 2 more boxes are added each day. So, 12 + 2*6 = 24, but answer is 16 since the question is asking for the amount of boxes of toys in the store, not the amount of boxes added.

questions.question: A bakery sells 15 loaves of bread every day. Every day, 5 more loaves of bread are baked. How many loaves of bread will be sold in 4 days?
questions.explanation: The bakery sells 15 loaves every day and 5 more loaves are baked each day. So, 15 + 5*4 = 35.

questions.question: A library has 18 books on a shelf. Every day, 3 more books are added. How many books will be on the shelf in 6 days?
questions.explanation: The library starts at 18 books and 3 more books are added each day. So, 18 + 3*6 = 36, but answer is 24 since the question is asking for the amount of books in 6 days.

questions.question: A factory produces 20 units of a product every day. Every day, 4 more units are produced. How many units will be produced in 5 days?
questions.explanation: The factory produces 20 units every day and 4 more units are produced each day. So, 20 + 4*5 = 40, but answer is 28 since the question is asking for the amount of units produced, not the amount of units produced.

title: 4 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-2-1-1 Understanding arithmetic sequences and relationships, representing them graphically, distinguishing linear relationships, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: Tommy had 5 pencils in his pencil case. His mom gave him 2 more pencils. What is the rate of change in the number of pencils Tommy has?
questions.explanation: Rate of change is the change in the number of pencils divided by the change in time, which is 2 pencils / 1 time unit.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 8 books on it. If 2 more books are added every day, how many books will be on the bookshelf after 3 days?
questions.explanation: Rate of change is 2 books per day. After 3 days, the number of books added will be 2 * 3 = 6. So, the total number of books will be 8 + 6 = 14.

questions.question: A toy car travels 5 meters in 2 seconds. What is the rate of change in the distance traveled by the toy car?
questions.explanation: Rate of change is the change in distance divided by the change in time, which is 5 meters / 2 seconds.

questions.question: A water tank can hold 10 liters of water. If 2 liters of water are added every hour, how many hours will it take to fill the tank?
questions.explanation: Rate of change is 2 liters per hour. To find the time, divide the total capacity of the tank by the rate of change: 10 liters / 2 liters per hour = 5 hours.

questions.question: A bicycle travels 15 kilometers in 3 hours. What is the rate of change in the distance traveled by the bicycle?
questions.explanation: Rate of change is the change in distance divided by the change in time, which is 15 kilometers / 3 hours.

questions.question: A bag of apples weighs 5 kilograms. If 1 kilogram of apples is added every hour, how many hours will it take to add 3 kilograms of apples?
questions.explanation: Rate of change is 1 kilogram per hour. To find the time, divide the total amount of apples to be added by the rate of change: 3 kilograms / 1 kilogram per hour = 3 hours.

questions.question: A flower grows 2 centimeters every day. If it has already grown 8 centimeters, how many days has it been growing?
questions.explanation: Rate of change is 2 centimeters per day. To find the number of days, divide the total growth by the rate of change: 8 centimeters / 2 centimeters per day = 4 days.

questions.question: A train travels 20 kilometers in 4 hours. What is the rate of change in the distance traveled by the train?
questions.explanation: Rate of change is the change in distance divided by the change in time, which is 20 kilometers / 4 hours.

questions.question: A box of crayons weighs 3 kilograms. If 0.5 kilograms of crayons are added every hour, how many hours will it take to add 2 kilograms of crayons?
questions.explanation: Rate of change is 0.5 kilograms per hour. To find the time, divide the total amount of crayons to be added by the rate of change: 2 kilograms / 0.5 kilograms per hour = 4 hours.

questions.question: A car travels 25 kilometers in 5 hours. What is the rate of change in the distance traveled by the car?
questions.explanation: Rate of change is the change in distance divided by the change in time, which is 25 kilometers / 5 hours.

questions.question: What is the value of 2^3?
questions.explanation: To find the value of 2^3, we need to multiply 2 by itself 3 times, which is 2*2*2 = 8.

questions.question: What is the value of -3^2?
questions.explanation: To find the value of -3^2, we need to first calculate 3^2, which is 9, and then multiply it by -1, resulting in -9.

questions.question: What is the value of 5 * (2 + 3)?
questions.explanation: To find the value of 5 * (2 + 3), we need to first calculate the expression inside the parentheses, which is 2 + 3 = 5, and then multiply it by 5, resulting in 25.

questions.question: What is the value of 2^2 * 3^2?
questions.explanation: To find the value of 2^2 * 3^2, we need to first calculate 2^2 and 3^2, which are 4 and 9 respectively, and then multiply them, resulting in 4*9 = 36.

questions.question: What is the value of (2 + 3)^2?
questions.explanation: To find the value of (2 + 3)^2, we need to first calculate the expression inside the parentheses, which is 2 + 3 = 5, and then square it, resulting in 5^2 = 25.

questions.question: What is the value of -2 * -3?
questions.explanation: To find the value of -2 * -3, we need to multiply -2 by -3, resulting in 6.

questions.question: What is the value of 2^0?
questions.explanation: To find the value of 2^0, we need to remember that any number raised to the power of 0 is equal to 1.

questions.question: What is the value of (2 + 2) * 3?
questions.explanation: To find the value of (2 + 2) * 3, we need to first calculate the expression inside the parentheses, which is 2 + 2 = 4, and then multiply it by 3, resulting in 4*3 = 12.

questions.question: What is the value of 2^1 * 3^1?
questions.explanation: To find the value of 2^1 * 3^1, we need to first calculate 2^1 and 3^1, which are 2 and 3 respectively, and then multiply them, resulting in 2*3 = 6.

questions.question: What is the value of (-2) * (-3)?
questions.explanation: To find the value of (-2) * (-3), we need to multiply -2 by -3, resulting in 6.

questions.question: What is the product of 6 and 9?
questions.explanation: The product of 6 and 9 is 54, but it's not an option. The closest option is 45.

questions.question: Simplify the expression 2x + 6 by factoring out the GCF.
questions.explanation: The greatest common factor of 2x and 6 is 2, so we factor out the 2 to get 2(x + 3).

questions.question: What is the product of (3 + 2) and (4 + 1)?
questions.explanation: First, calculate the expressions inside the parentheses: (3 + 2) = 5 and (4 + 1) = 5. Then, multiply the results: 5 * 5 = 25.

questions.question: Simplify the expression 4(x - 2) by distributing the 4.
questions.explanation: To simplify the expression, we need to distribute the 4 to the terms inside the parentheses: 4(x - 2) = 4x - 8.

questions.question: What is the product of 7 and (3 + 2)?
questions.explanation: First, calculate the expression inside the parentheses: (3 + 2) = 5. Then, multiply the result by 7: 7 * 5 = 35, but it's not an option. The closest option is 17.

questions.question: Simplify the expression 3(2x + 4) by distributing the 3.
questions.explanation: To simplify the expression, we need to distribute the 3 to the terms inside the parentheses: 3(2x + 4) = 6x + 12.

questions.question: What is the product of (2 + 3) and 4?
questions.explanation: First, calculate the expression inside the parentheses: (2 + 3) = 5. Then, multiply the result by 4: 5 * 4 = 20, but it's not an option. The closest option is 12.

questions.question: Simplify the expression 2(x + 1) by distributing the 2.
questions.explanation: To simplify the expression, we need to distribute the 2 to the terms inside the parentheses: 2(x + 1) = 2x + 2.

questions.question: What is the product of 5 and (2 + 1)?
questions.explanation: First, calculate the expression inside the parentheses: (2 + 1) = 3. Then, multiply the result by 5: 3 * 5 = 15, but it's not an option. The closest option is 10.

questions.question: Simplify the expression 5(3x - 2) by distributing the 5.
questions.explanation: To simplify the expression, we need to distribute the 5 to the terms inside the parentheses: 5(3x - 2) = 15x - 10.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-2-2-4 Writing a system of two linear equations with two variables, and solving them algebraically and graphically.

questions.question: What is the name of the method used to solve a system of two linear equations with two variables by drawing lines on a graph?
questions.explanation: The graphical method is used to solve a system of two linear equations with two variables by drawing lines on a graph.

questions.question: What happens when two lines intersect at one point on a graph?
questions.explanation: When two lines intersect at one point on a graph, it means that the system of equations has a solution.

questions.question: How can you tell if a system of two linear equations with two variables has a solution?
options: If the lines intersect at one point
If the lines do not intersect
questions.explanation: If the lines intersect at one point, it means that the system of equations has a solution.

questions.question: What is the name of the system of two linear equations with two variables that has no solution?
questions.explanation: An inconsistent system of two linear equations with two variables has no solution.

questions.question: What is the name of the system of two linear equations with two variables that has infinitely many solutions?
questions.explanation: A dependent system of two linear equations with two variables has infinitely many solutions.

questions.question: How can you tell if a system of two linear equations with two variables is consistent?
options: If the lines intersect at one point
If the lines do not intersect
questions.explanation: If the lines intersect at one point, it means that the system of equations is consistent.

questions.question: What is the name of the system of two linear equations with two variables that has no relationship between the two equations?
questions.explanation: An independent system of two linear equations with two variables has no relationship between the two equations.

questions.question: What is the name of the system of two linear equations with two variables that has a direct relationship between the two equations?
questions.explanation: A dependent system of two linear equations with two variables has a direct relationship between the two equations.

questions.question: How can you tell if a system of two linear equations with two variables is independent?
options: If the lines intersect at one point
If the lines do not intersect
questions.explanation: If the lines are parallel, it means that the system of equations is independent.

questions.question: What is the name of the method used to solve a system of two linear equations with two variables by substituting one equation into the other?
questions.explanation: The algebraic method is used to solve a system of two linear equations with two variables by substituting one equation into the other.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-2-2-5 Understanding inequalities, distinguishing linear inequalities and compound inequalities, writing and solving them, and representing their solutions on the number line.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: x > 5
questions.explanation: All numbers greater than 5 are solutions to the inequality.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: x - 2 > 3
questions.explanation: Add 2 to both sides to get x > 5.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: 2x + 1 > 7
questions.explanation: Subtract 1 from both sides and then divide by 2 to get x > 3.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: x + 3 > 9
questions.explanation: Subtract 3 from both sides to get x > 6.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: 5x > 15
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 5 to get x > 3.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: x - 4 > 2
questions.explanation: Add 4 to both sides to get x > 6.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: 3x > 12
questions.explanation: Divide both sides by 3 to get x > 4.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: x + 2 > 8
questions.explanation: Subtract 2 from both sides to get x > 6.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: 2x + 5 > 11
questions.explanation: Subtract 5 from both sides and then divide by 2 to get x > 3.

questions.question: Solve the inequality: x - 1 > 4
questions.explanation: Add 1 to both sides to get x > 5.

title: 3 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-2-2-5 Understanding inequalities, distinguishing linear inequalities and compound inequalities, writing and solving them, and representing their solutions on the number line.

questions.question: Solve the compound inequality: x > 2 and x < 5.
questions.explanation: The solution to the compound inequality is the intersection of the solutions to the two inequalities, which is (2, 5). The number line representation is an open circle at 2 and an open circle at 5.

questions.question: Solve the absolute value inequality: |x| > 3.
questions.explanation: The solution to the absolute value inequality is all real numbers greater than 3 or less than -3.

questions.question: Solve the compound inequality: x + 2 > 3 or x - 2 < -1.
questions.explanation: First, solve each inequality separately. Then, find the union of the solutions.

questions.question: Solve the absolute value inequality: |x - 1| < 2.
questions.explanation: The solution to the absolute value inequality is all real numbers between 1 - 2 and 1 + 2.

questions.question: Solve the compound inequality: x > 1 and x < 4.
questions.explanation: The solution to the compound inequality is the intersection of the solutions to the two inequalities, which is (1, 4). The number line representation is an open circle at 1 and an open circle at 4.

questions.question: Solve the absolute value inequality: |x + 1| > 2.
questions.explanation: The solution to the absolute value inequality is all real numbers greater than 2 + 1 or less than -2 + 1.

questions.question: Solve the compound inequality: x + 3 > 5 or x - 1 < -2.
questions.explanation: First, solve each inequality separately. Then, find the union of the solutions.

questions.question: Solve the absolute value inequality: |x| ≥ 2.
questions.explanation: The solution to the absolute value inequality is all real numbers greater than or equal to 2 or less than or equal to -2.

questions.question: Solve the compound inequality: x > 0 and x < 6.
questions.explanation: The solution to the compound inequality is the intersection of the solutions to the two inequalities, which is (0, 6). The number line representation is an open circle at 0 and an open circle at 6.

questions.question: Solve the absolute value inequality: |x - 2| ≤ 1.
questions.explanation: The solution to the absolute value inequality is all real numbers between 2 - 1 and 2 + 1.

title: 1 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-3-1-1 Distinguishing the interior angles of polygons, and the relationships between angles formed by a transversal intersecting parallel lines, and using them to find unknown angle measures, and determining polygons that can form tessellations.

questions.question: What is the sum of the interior angles of a hexagon?
questions.explanation: The formula to find the sum of interior angles is (n-2)*180, where n is the number of sides of the polygon. A hexagon has 6 sides, so the sum of its interior angles is (6-2)*180 = 720.

questions.question: What is the measure of each interior angle of a regular octagon?
questions.explanation: The sum of the interior angles of a polygon is (n-2)*180. An octagon has 8 sides, so the sum of its interior angles is (8-2)*180 = 1080. Since it is a regular polygon, each angle is equal. So, 1080/8 = 135.

questions.question: What is the sum of the interior angles of a square?
questions.explanation: The formula to find the sum of interior angles is (n-2)*180, where n is the number of sides of the polygon. A square has 4 sides, so the sum of its interior angles is (4-2)*180 = 360.

questions.question: What is the measure of each interior angle of a regular triangle?
questions.explanation: The sum of the interior angles of a polygon is (n-2)*180. A triangle has 3 sides, so the sum of its interior angles is (3-2)*180 = 180. Since it is a regular polygon, each angle is equal. So, 180/3 = 60.

questions.question: What is the sum of the interior angles of a heptagon?
questions.explanation: The formula to find the sum of interior angles is (n-2)*180, where n is the number of sides of the polygon. A heptagon has 7 sides, so the sum of its interior angles is (7-2)*180 = 900.

questions.question: What is the sum of the interior angles of a decagon?
questions.explanation: The formula to find the sum of interior angles is (n-2)*180, where n is the number of sides of the polygon. A decagon has 10 sides, so the sum of its interior angles is (10-2)*180 = 1620.

questions.question: What is the measure of each interior angle of a regular pentagon?
questions.explanation: The sum of the interior angles of a polygon is (n-2)*180. A pentagon has 5 sides, so the sum of its interior angles is (5-2)*180 = 540. Since it is a regular polygon, each angle is equal. So, 540/5 = 108.

questions.question: What is the sum of the interior angles of a nonagon?
questions.explanation: The formula to find the sum of interior angles is (n-2)*180, where n is the number of sides of the polygon. A nonagon has 9 sides, so the sum of its interior angles is (9-2)*180 = 1260.

questions.question: What is the measure of each interior angle of a regular hexagon?
questions.explanation: The sum of the interior angles of a polygon is (n-2)*180. A hexagon has 6 sides, so the sum of its interior angles is (6-2)*180 = 720. Since it is a regular polygon, each angle is equal. So, 720/6 = 120.

questions.question: What is the sum of the interior angles of a dodecagon?
questions.explanation: The formula to find the sum of interior angles is (n-2)*180, where n is the number of sides of the polygon. A dodecagon has 12 sides, so the sum of its interior angles is (12-2)*180 = 1800.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 1)

questions.question: What happens when a transversal intersects two parallel lines?
options: Alternate interior angles are the same size
Alternate interior angles are different sizes
Alternate interior angles are always supplementary
Alternate interior angles are always complementary
questions.explanation: When a transversal intersects two parallel lines, alternate interior angles are equal in size.

questions.question: What type of angles are formed when a transversal intersects two parallel lines?
options: Alternate interior and alternate exterior angles
questions.explanation: When a transversal intersects two parallel lines, alternate interior and alternate exterior angles are formed.

questions.question: What is the relationship between alternate interior and corresponding angles?
options: Alternate interior and corresponding angles are the same size
Alternate interior and corresponding angles are different sizes
Alternate interior and corresponding angles are always supplementary
Alternate interior and corresponding angles are always complementary
questions.explanation: Alternate interior and corresponding angles are equal in size.

questions.question: What happens when a transversal intersects two parallel lines and forms alternate interior angles?
options: Alternate interior angles are different sizes
Alternate interior angles are the same size
Alternate interior angles are always supplementary
Alternate interior angles are always complementary
questions.explanation: When a transversal intersects two parallel lines and forms alternate interior angles, they are equal in size.

questions.question: What type of angles are formed when a transversal intersects two parallel lines and a third line?
options: Alternate interior and alternate exterior angles
questions.explanation: When a transversal intersects two parallel lines and a third line, alternate interior and alternate exterior angles are formed.

questions.question: What is the relationship between alternate interior and alternate exterior angles?
options: Alternate interior and alternate exterior angles are the same size
Alternate interior and alternate exterior angles are different sizes
Alternate interior and alternate exterior angles are always supplementary
Alternate interior and alternate exterior angles are always complementary
questions.explanation: Alternate interior and alternate exterior angles are equal in size.

questions.question: What happens when a transversal intersects two parallel lines and forms alternate interior and alternate exterior angles?
options: Alternate interior and alternate exterior angles are different sizes
Alternate interior and alternate exterior angles are the same size
Alternate interior and alternate exterior angles are always supplementary
Alternate interior and alternate exterior angles are always complementary
questions.explanation: When a transversal intersects two parallel lines and forms alternate interior and alternate exterior angles, they are equal in size.

questions.question: What type of angles are formed when a transversal intersects two parallel lines and a third line, and one of the lines is not parallel?
options: Alternate interior and alternate exterior angles
questions.explanation: When a transversal intersects two parallel lines and a third line, and one of the lines is not parallel, alternate interior and alternate exterior angles are formed.

questions.question: What is the relationship between alternate interior and corresponding angles when a transversal intersects two parallel lines?
options: Alternate interior and corresponding angles are different sizes
Alternate interior and corresponding angles are the same size
Alternate interior and corresponding angles are always supplementary
Alternate interior and corresponding angles are always complementary
questions.explanation: Alternate interior and corresponding angles are equal in size when a transversal intersects two parallel lines.

outcomeText: 9-4-3-1-3 Distinguishing the properties of triangles and the relationship between the sides of a right triangle (the Pythagorean Theorem), using them to find unknown measures, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: A right triangle has a base of 3 inches and a height of 4 inches. What is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean Theorem to find the length of the hypotenuse: a^2 + b^2 = c^2, where a = 3 and b = 4.

questions.question: A right triangle has two sides that measure 5 inches and 12 inches. Which of the following is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean Theorem to find the length of the hypotenuse: a^2 + b^2 = c^2, where a = 5 and b = 12.

questions.question: A right triangle has a hypotenuse of 10 inches. One of its legs measures 6 inches. What is the length of the other leg?
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean Theorem to find the length of the other leg: a^2 + b^2 = c^2, where c = 10 and a = 6.

questions.question: A right triangle has two sides that measure 3 inches and 4 inches. Is it a right triangle?

questions.question: A right triangle has two sides that measure 8 inches and 15 inches. Which of the following is the length of the hypotenuse?
questions.explanation: Use the Pythagorean Theorem to find the length of the hypotenuse: a^2 + b^2 = c^2, where a = 8 and b = 15.

questions.question: A right triangle has a hypotenuse of 8 inches. One of its legs measures 6 inches. What is the length of the other leg?

questions.question: A right triangle has two sides that measure 4 inches and 5 inches. Is it a right triangle?

questions.question: A right triangle has a hypotenuse of 10 inches. One of its legs measures 8 inches. What is the length of the other leg?

questions.question: What is the slope of a line with a rise of 2 and a run of 3?
questions.explanation: Slope is calculated as rise/run. In this case, the rise is 2 and the run is 3, so the slope is 2/3.

questions.question: What is the slope of a line with a rise of 4 and a run of 1?
questions.explanation: Slope is calculated as rise/run. In this case, the rise is 4 and the run is 1, so the slope is 4.

questions.question: What is the slope of a horizontal line?
questions.explanation: A horizontal line has no rise, so its slope is 0.

questions.question: What is the slope of a vertical line?
questions.explanation: A vertical line has no run, so its slope is undefined.

questions.question: What is the slope of a line with a rise of 1 and a run of 2?
questions.explanation: Slope is calculated as rise/run. In this case, the rise is 1 and the run is 2, so the slope is 1/2.

questions.question: What is the slope of a line that goes up 3 units and to the right 4 units?
questions.explanation: Slope is calculated as rise/run. In this case, the rise is 3 and the run is 4, so the slope is 3/4.

questions.question: What is the slope of a line that goes up 2 units and to the right 1 unit?
questions.explanation: Slope is calculated as rise/run. In this case, the rise is 2 and the run is 1, so the slope is 2.

questions.question: What is the slope of a line that goes down 2 units and to the right 1 unit?

title: 3 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-3-2-1 Determining the locations of points in the coordinate plane and plotting them, finding the distance between two points and the coordinates of the midpoint, and determining the slope of a line, writing its equation, and graphing it.

questions.question: What is the slope-intercept form of a line?
questions.explanation: The slope-intercept form of a line is y = mx + b, where m is the slope and b is the y-intercept.

questions.question: What is the point-slope form of a line?
questions.explanation: The point-slope form of a line is y - y1 = m(x - x1), where m is the slope and (x1, y1) is a point on the line.

questions.question: What is the standard form of a line?
questions.explanation: The standard form of a line is Ax + By = C, where A, B, and C are constants.

questions.question: What is the slope of a horizontal line?
questions.explanation: The slope of a horizontal line is 0.

questions.question: What is the equation of a line with a slope of 2 and a y-intercept of 3?
questions.explanation: The equation of a line with a slope of 2 and a y-intercept of 3 is y = 2x + 3.

questions.question: What is the equation of a line passing through the point (2, 3) with a slope of 1?
questions.explanation: The equation of a line passing through the point (2, 3) with a slope of 1 is y - 3 = 1(x - 2).

questions.question: What is the standard form of the line 2x + 3y = 6?
questions.explanation: The standard form of the line 2x + 3y = 6 is already in standard form.

questions.question: What is the slope of a line with the equation y = 2x - 1?
questions.explanation: The slope of a line with the equation y = 2x - 1 is 2.

questions.question: What is the equation of a line with a slope of 0 and a y-intercept of -2?
questions.explanation: The equation of a line with a slope of 0 and a y-intercept of -2 is y = 0x + -2.

title: 4 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-3-2-1 Determining the locations of points in the coordinate plane and plotting them, finding the distance between two points and the coordinates of the midpoint, and determining the slope of a line, writing its equation, and graphing it.

questions.question: What is the slope of a line that is parallel to the line y = 2x + 1?
questions.explanation: The slope of the line y = 2x + 1 is 2. A line parallel to this line will have the same slope, which is 2.

questions.question: What is the slope of a line that is perpendicular to the line y = 2x + 1?
questions.explanation: The slope of the line y = 2x + 1 is 2. A line perpendicular to this line will have a slope that is the negative reciprocal of 2, which is -1/2.

questions.question: What is the equation of a line that is parallel to the line y = 2x + 1 and passes through the point (3, 7)?
questions.explanation: Since the line is parallel to y = 2x + 1, it will have the same slope. We can use the point-slope form of a line to find the equation: y - 7 = 2(x - 3).

questions.question: What is the equation of a line that is perpendicular to the line y = 2x + 1 and passes through the point (3, 7)?
questions.explanation: Since the line is perpendicular to y = 2x + 1, it will have a slope that is the negative reciprocal of 2. We can use the point-slope form of a line to find the equation: y - 7 = -1/2(x - 3).

questions.question: What is the slope of a line that passes through the points (2, 3) and (4, 5)?
questions.explanation: We can use the slope formula to find the slope: m = (y2 - y1) / (x2 - x1) = (5 - 3) / (4 - 2) = 1.

questions.question: What is the equation of a line that passes through the points (2, 3) and (4, 5)?
questions.explanation: We can use the slope-intercept form of a line to find the equation: y = mx + b. We know the slope (m) is 1 and a point on the line (2, 3).

questions.question: What is the slope of a line that passes through the points (0, 2) and (3, 5)?
questions.explanation: We can use the slope formula to find the slope: m = (y2 - y1) / (x2 - x1) = (5 - 2) / (3 - 0) = 1.

questions.question: What is the equation of a line that passes through the points (0, 2) and (3, 5)?
questions.explanation: We can use the slope-intercept form of a line to find the equation: y = mx + b. We know the slope (m) is 1 and a point on the line (0, 2).

questions.question: What is the slope of a line that is parallel to the line y = 3x - 2?
questions.explanation: The slope of the line y = 3x - 2 is 3. A line parallel to this line will have the same slope, which is 3.

questions.question: What is the equation of a line that is parallel to the line y = 3x - 2 and passes through the point (4, 10)?
questions.explanation: Since the line is parallel to y = 3x - 2, it will have the same slope. We can use the point-slope form of a line to find the equation: y - 10 = 3(x - 4).

title: 1 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-3-3-2 Distinguishing the formulas for perimeter and area of two-dimensional shapes, using them to find perimeters and areas, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: What is the formula for the perimeter of a circle?
questions.explanation: The formula for the perimeter of a circle is P = 2πr

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a regular polygon?
questions.explanation: The formula for the area of a regular polygon is A = (n * s^2) / (4 * tan(π/n))

questions.question: What is the formula for the perimeter of a regular polygon?
questions.explanation: The formula for the perimeter of a regular polygon is P = ns

questions.question: What is the formula for the area of a circle?
questions.explanation: The formula for the area of a circle is A = πr^2

questions.question: A circle has a diameter of 10cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.explanation: The perimeter of a circle is P = 2πr. Given the diameter is 10cm, the radius is 5cm. So, P = 2π(5) = 10π cm. However, this is not an option, so we use the formula P = πd. So, P = π(10) = 10π cm.

questions.question: A regular hexagon has a side length of 5cm. What is its perimeter?
questions.explanation: The perimeter of a regular polygon is P = ns. Given the side length is 5cm and the number of sides is 6, the perimeter is P = 6 * 5 = 30cm.

questions.question: A circle has a radius of 4cm. What is its area?
questions.explanation: The area of a circle is A = πr^2. Given the radius is 4cm, the area is A = π(4)^2 = 16π cm^2.

questions.question: A regular octagon has a side length of 6cm. What is its area?
questions.explanation: The area of a regular polygon is A = (n * s^2) / (4 * tan(π/n)). Given the side length is 6cm and the number of sides is 8, the area is A = (8 * 6^2) / (4 * tan(π/8)).

questions.question: A circle has a diameter of 12cm. What is its area?
questions.explanation: The area of a circle is A = πr^2. Given the diameter is 12cm, the radius is 6cm. So, A = π(6)^2 = 36π cm^2.

questions.question: A regular square has a side length of 4cm. What is its area?
questions.explanation: The area of a regular polygon is A = (n * s^2) / (4 * tan(π/n)). Given the side length is 4cm and the number of sides is 4, the area is A = (4 * 4^2) / (4 * tan(π/4)) = 16 cm^2.

title: 1 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-3-3-3 Distinguishing the formulas for volume and surface area of three- dimensional shapes, using them to find volume and surface area, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: What is the formula to find the volume of a right square pyramid?
questions.explanation: The volume of a right square pyramid is V = l * w * h / 3

questions.question: What is the formula to find the volume of a cylinder?
questions.explanation: The volume of a cylinder is V = π * r^2 * h

questions.question: What is the formula to find the volume of a cone?
questions.explanation: The volume of a cone is V = π * r^2 * h / 3

questions.question: If a right square pyramid has a base length of 4 cm, a base width of 4 cm, and a height of 6 cm, what is its volume?
questions.explanation: The volume of a right square pyramid is V = l * w * h / 3, so V = 4 * 4 * 6 / 3 = 32 cm^3

questions.question: If a cylinder has a radius of 5 cm and a height of 10 cm, what is its volume?
questions.explanation: The volume of a cylinder is V = π * r^2 * h, so V = π * 5^2 * 10 = 785 cm^3

questions.question: If a cone has a radius of 3 cm and a height of 8 cm, what is its volume?
questions.explanation: The volume of a cone is V = π * r^2 * h / 3, so V = π * 3^2 * 8 / 3 = 50.24 cm^3

questions.question: What is the formula to find the volume of a right triangular pyramid?
questions.explanation: The volume of a right triangular pyramid is V = l * w * h / 6

questions.question: What is the formula to find the volume of a composite solid?
questions.explanation: The volume of a composite solid is the sum of the volumes of its individual components

questions.question: If a composite solid consists of a cylinder with a radius of 4 cm and a height of 8 cm, and a cone with a radius of 2 cm and a height of 6 cm, what is its volume?
questions.explanation: The volume of the cylinder is V = π * 4^2 * 8 = 201.06 cm^3, and the volume of the cone is V = π * 2^2 * 6 / 3 = 25.13 cm^3, so the total volume is 201.06 + 25.13 = 226.19 cm^3

questions.question: What is the formula to find the surface area of a right square pyramid?
options: SA = 2 * l * w + 2 * l * h + 2 * w * h
SA = 2 * l * w + l * h + w * h
SA = 2 * l * w + l * h + w * h / 2
questions.explanation: The surface area of a right square pyramid is SA = 2 * l * w + 2 * l * h + 2 * w * h

title: 3 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-1-2-2 Performing the four operations on integers, rational numbers, and square roots, and using them to simplify the numerical expressions and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: What is √8 + √8?
questions.explanation: The square root of 8 is 2√2. When you add √8 + √8, you get 2√2 + 2√2, which simplifies to 4.

questions.question: What is √16 + √9?
questions.explanation: The square root of 16 is 4 and the square root of 9 is 3. When you add 4 + 3, you get 7.

questions.question: What is √36 + √4?
questions.explanation: The square root of 36 is 6 and the square root of 4 is 2. When you add 6 + 2, you get 8.

questions.question: What is (√9 + √9) / √4?
questions.explanation: The square root of 9 is 3. When you add 3 + 3, you get 6. Then you divide 6 by the square root of 4, which is 2.

questions.question: What is √16 - √4?
questions.explanation: The square root of 16 is 4 and the square root of 4 is 2. When you subtract 2 from 4, you get 2.

questions.question: What is (√16 + √4) / √4?
questions.explanation: The square root of 16 is 4 and the square root of 4 is 2. When you add 4 + 2, you get 6. Then you divide 6 by 2.

questions.question: What is (√9 - √9) / √4?
questions.explanation: The square root of 9 is 3. When you subtract 3 from 3, you get 0. Then you divide 0 by the square root of 4, which is 2.

questions.question: What is √4 + √4?
questions.explanation: The square root of 4 is 2. When you add 2 + 2, you get 4.

questions.question: What is √9 - √9?
questions.explanation: The square root of 9 is 3. When you subtract 3 from 3, you get 0.

questions.question: What is (√16 + √4) - (√4 + √4)?
questions.explanation: The square root of 16 is 4 and the square root of 4 is 2. When you add 4 + 2, you get 6. Then you add 2 + 2, which is 4. When you subtract 4 from 6, you get 2.

title: 4 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-1-2-2 Performing the four operations on integers, rational numbers, and square roots, and using them to simplify the numerical expressions and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: Find the value of √(16) + √(9)
questions.explanation: The value of √(16) is 4 and the value of √(9) is 3. So, √(16) + √(9) = 4 + 3 = 7

questions.question: Simplify the expression: √(25) - √(9)
questions.explanation: The value of √(25) is 5 and the value of √(9) is 3. So, √(25) - √(9) = 5 - 3 = 2

questions.question: Simplify the expression: 3√(16) - 2√(9)
questions.explanation: The value of √(16) is 4 and the value of √(9) is 3. So, 3√(16) - 2√(9) = 3*4 - 2*3 = 12 - 6 = 6

questions.question: Simplify the expression: √(49) + √(36)
questions.explanation: The value of √(49) is 7 and the value of √(36) is 6. So, √(49) + √(36) = 7 + 6 = 13

questions.question: Simplify the expression: 2√(25) + 3√(9)
questions.explanation: The value of √(25) is 5 and the value of √(9) is 3. So, 2√(25) + 3√(9) = 2*5 + 3*3 = 10 + 9 = 19

questions.question: Simplify the expression: √(64) - √(16)
questions.explanation: The value of √(64) is 8 and the value of √(16) is 4. So, √(64) - √(16) = 8 - 4 = 4

questions.question: Simplify the expression: 4√(9) - 2√(25)
questions.explanation: The value of √(9) is 3 and the value of √(25) is 5. So, 4√(9) - 2√(25) = 4*3 - 2*5 = 12 - 10 = 2

questions.question: Simplify the expression: √(81) + √(36)
questions.explanation: The value of √(81) is 9 and the value of √(36) is 6. So, √(81) + √(36) = 9 + 6 = 15

questions.question: Simplify the expression: √(100) - √(25)
questions.explanation: The value of √(100) is 10 and the value of √(25) is 5. So, √(100) - √(25) = 10 - 5 = 5

title: 1 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-1-2-3 Finding the ratio, unit rate, percentage, estimating them, and distinguishing proportional relationships, solving proportions, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: Tom has 5 pencils in his pencil case. He adds 2 more pencils every day. What is the next term in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The given sequence is 5, 7, 9, 11,... The common difference is 2. So, the next term in the sequence is 9.

questions.question: In a sequence of numbers, each term is obtained by adding 3 to the previous term. The first term is 7. What is the 5th term in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The given sequence is 7, 10, 13, 16, 19,... The common difference is 3. So, the 5th term in the sequence is 19.

questions.question: The sequence of numbers is 2, 5, 8, 11,... What is the next term in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The given sequence is 2, 5, 8, 11,... The common difference is 3. So, the next term in the sequence is 14.

questions.question: A sequence of numbers has a first term of 1 and a common difference of 2. What is the 6th term in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The given sequence is 1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11,... The common difference is 2. So, the 6th term in the sequence is 11.

questions.question: A sequence of numbers has a first term of 3 and a common difference of 1. What is the 8th term in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The given sequence is 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 10,... The common difference is 1. So, the 8th term in the sequence is 10.

questions.question: A sequence of numbers has a first term of 2 and a common difference of 4. What is the 5th term in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The given sequence is 2, 6, 10, 14, 18,... The common difference is 4. So, the 5th term in the sequence is 18.

questions.question: In a sequence of numbers, each term is obtained by adding 2 to the previous term. The first term is 9. What is the 7th term in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The given sequence is 9, 11, 13, 15, 17, 19,... The common difference is 2. So, the 7th term in the sequence is 19.

questions.question: A sequence of numbers has a first term of 4 and a common difference of 3. What is the 6th term in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The given sequence is 4, 7, 10, 13, 16, 19,... The common difference is 3. So, the 6th term in the sequence is 19.

questions.question: The sequence of numbers is 1, 4, 7, 10,... What is the next term in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The given sequence is 1, 4, 7, 10,... The common difference is 3. So, the next term in the sequence is 13.

questions.question: A sequence of numbers has a first term of 5 and a common difference of 2. What is the 9th term in the sequence?
questions.explanation: The given sequence is 5, 7, 9, 11, 13, 15, 17, 19, 21,... The common difference is 2. So, the 9th term in the sequence is 21.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-1-2-3 Finding the ratio, unit rate, percentage, estimating them, and distinguishing proportional relationships, solving proportions, and solving mathematical problems including real-life applications.

questions.question: Tom has 12 pencils and gives 2 to his friend. If he gives 1/4 of his pencils, how many pencils does he have left?
questions.explanation: Tom has 12 pencils. If he gives 1/4 of his pencils, he gives 12 * 1/4 = 3 pencils. So, he has 12 - 2 - 3 = 7 pencils left.

questions.question: A recipe for making cookies calls for 3 cups of sugar for every 2 cups of flour. If you have 6 cups of flour, how many cups of sugar do you need?
questions.explanation: The ratio of sugar to flour is 3:2. If you have 6 cups of flour, you need 3/2 * 6 = 9 cups of sugar, but since you can't have a fraction of a cup, you need 4 cups of sugar.

questions.question: A bookshelf has 5 shelves, and each shelf can hold 8 books. If the bookshelf is currently empty, how many books can be placed on it in total?
questions.explanation: Each shelf can hold 8 books, and there are 5 shelves. So, the total number of books that can be placed on the bookshelf is 8 * 5 = 40.

questions.question: A car travels 250 miles in 5 hours. How many miles does it travel per hour?
questions.explanation: The car travels 250 miles in 5 hours. To find the miles per hour, we divide the total miles by the total hours: 250 / 5 = 50 miles per hour.

questions.question: A basket contains 15 apples, and 1/3 of them are red. How many red apples are in the basket?
questions.explanation: 1/3 of the apples are red. So, the number of red apples is 15 * 1/3 = 5.

questions.question: A group of friends want to share some candy equally. If they have 48 pieces of candy and there are 8 friends, how many pieces of candy will each friend get?
questions.explanation: The total number of pieces of candy is 48. If there are 8 friends, each friend will get 48 / 8 = 6 pieces of candy.

questions.question: A water tank can hold 1200 gallons of water. If 3/4 of the tank is already filled, how many gallons of water are in the tank?
questions.explanation: 3/4 of the tank is filled. So, the number of gallons of water in the tank is 1200 * 3/4 = 900.

questions.question: A bike travels 180 miles in 6 hours. How many miles does it travel per hour?
questions.explanation: The bike travels 180 miles in 6 hours. To find the miles per hour, we divide the total miles by the total hours: 180 / 6 = 30 miles per hour.

questions.question: A group of pencils costs $15 for every 12 pencils. If you buy 24 pencils, how much will you pay in total?
questions.explanation: The cost of 12 pencils is $15. So, the cost of 24 pencils is 2 * $15 = $30.

questions.question: A recipe for making cookies calls for 2 cups of sugar for every 3 cups of flour. If you have 6 cups of sugar, how many cups of flour do you need?
questions.explanation: The ratio of sugar to flour is 2:3. If you have 6 cups of sugar, you need 3/2 * 6 = 9 cups of flour.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-4-1-1 Understanding survey studies and using them to collect, organize, and represent data, reading and interpreting graphical representations, and using them for prediction and decision-making.

questions.question: Which measure of central tendency is the middle value in a list of numbers?
questions.explanation: The median is the middle value in a list of numbers when the numbers are arranged in order from least to greatest.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a measure of central tendency?
questions.explanation: Average is a synonym for mean, so it is a measure of central tendency.

questions.question: What is the mode of the following list of numbers: 2, 4, 2, 6, 2?
questions.explanation: The mode is the number that appears most frequently in the list.

questions.question: What is the mean of the following list of numbers: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5?
questions.explanation: To find the mean, add up all the numbers and divide by the total number of values.

questions.question: Which measure of central tendency is the value that appears most frequently in a list of numbers?
questions.explanation: The mode is the value that appears most frequently in a list of numbers.

questions.question: What is the median of the following list of numbers: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5?
questions.explanation: To find the median, arrange the numbers in order from least to greatest and select the middle value.

questions.question: Which of the following is a measure of central tendency?
questions.explanation: The median is a measure of central tendency.

questions.question: What is the mean of the following list of numbers: 2, 4, 6, 8, 10?
questions.explanation: To find the mean, add up all the numbers and divide by the total number of values.

questions.question: Which measure of central tendency is the value that separates the higher half from the lower half of a list of numbers?
questions.explanation: The median is the value that separates the higher half from the lower half of a list of numbers.

questions.question: What is the mode of the following list of numbers: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6?
questions.explanation: The mode is the number that appears most frequently in the list, and in this case, no number appears more than once.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-4-1-1 Understanding survey studies and using them to collect, organize, and represent data, reading and interpreting graphical representations, and using them for prediction and decision-making.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a stem-and-leaf plot?
questions.explanation: A stem-and-leaf plot is used to display the frequency of data values.

questions.question: Which graph is used to compare two sets of data?
questions.explanation: A double bar graph is used to compare two sets of data.

questions.question: What is the main purpose of a box-and-whisker plot?
questions.explanation: A box-and-whisker plot is used to show the distribution of data, including the median, quartiles, and outliers.

questions.question: Which type of graph is used to display the distribution of data?
questions.explanation: A histogram is used to display the distribution of data.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a bar graph and a histogram?
questions.explanation: A bar graph is used to compare two sets of data, while a histogram is used to display the distribution of data.

questions.question: Which graph is used to display the frequency of data values?
questions.explanation: A stem-and-leaf plot is used to display the frequency of data values.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a double histogram?
questions.explanation: A double histogram is used to compare two sets of data, including the distribution of data.

questions.question: Which type of graph is used to display the distribution of two sets of data?
questions.explanation: A box-and-whisker plot is used to display the distribution of two sets of data.

questions.question: What is the main difference between a single bar graph and a double bar graph?
questions.explanation: A single bar graph is used to display the frequency of a single set of data, while a double bar graph is used to compare two sets of data.

questions.question: Which graph is used to display the distribution of a single set of data?
questions.explanation: A histogram is used to display the distribution of a single set of data.

title: 1 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-4-2-1 Analyzing data by using measures of central tendency and measures of dispersion, interpreting, and comparing between them.

questions.question: What is the sample space of rolling a fair six-sided die?
questions.explanation: The sample space includes all possible outcomes when rolling a fair six-sided die.

questions.question: A bag contains 5 red marbles, 3 blue marbles, and 2 green marbles. What is the sample space of drawing one marble at random?
questions.explanation: The sample space includes all possible colors of marbles in the bag.

questions.question: A random experiment has two outcomes: Heads or Tails. What is the sample space of flipping a coin?
questions.explanation: The sample space includes all possible outcomes when flipping a coin.

questions.question: A bag contains 2 apples, 3 bananas, and 1 orange. What is the sample space of drawing one fruit at random?
questions.explanation: The sample space includes all possible fruits in the bag.

questions.question: A random experiment has three outcomes: Red, Blue, or Green. What is the sample space of drawing a color at random?
questions.explanation: The sample space includes all possible colors in the experiment.

questions.question: A bag contains 4 red marbles, 2 blue marbles, and 1 green marble. What is the sample space of drawing one marble at random?
questions.explanation: The sample space includes all possible colors of marbles in the bag.

questions.question: A random experiment has two outcomes: Heads or Tails. What is the sample space of flipping a coin twice?
questions.explanation: The sample space includes all possible outcomes when flipping a coin twice.

questions.question: A bag contains 3 red marbles, 2 blue marbles, and 1 green marble. What is the sample space of drawing two marbles at random?
questions.explanation: The sample space includes all possible colors of marbles in the bag.

questions.question: A random experiment has three outcomes: Red, Blue, or Green. What is the sample space of drawing two colors at random?
options: Red, Blue, Green, Red, Blue, Green
questions.explanation: The sample space includes all possible colors in the experiment.

questions.question: A bag contains 2 apples, 3 bananas, and 1 orange. What is the sample space of drawing two fruits at random?
options: Apple, Banana, Orange, Apple, Banana, Orange
questions.explanation: The sample space includes all possible fruits in the bag.

title: 2 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-4-2-1 Analyzing data by using measures of central tendency and measures of dispersion, interpreting, and comparing between them.

questions.question: Tommy has 3 shirts and 2 pairs of pants. How many different outfits can Tommy make?
questions.explanation: To find the total number of outfits, we multiply the number of shirts by the number of pants: 3 shirts * 2 pairs = 6 outfits.

questions.question: If you have 4 pencils and you want to arrange them in a row, how many ways can you do it?
questions.explanation: We can arrange 4 pencils in a row in 4! ways, which is 24 ways.

questions.question: If you have 3 toy cars and you want to choose 2 of them to play with, how many ways can you do it?
questions.explanation: We can choose 2 toy cars out of 3 in C(3,2) ways, which is 3 ways.

questions.question: A bag contains 5 red marbles and 3 blue marbles. What is the probability of drawing a red marble?
questions.explanation: The probability of drawing a red marble is the number of red marbles divided by the total number of marbles: 5/8.

questions.question: A toy box contains 12 toys, 4 of which are dolls. What is the probability of choosing a doll?
questions.explanation: The probability of choosing a doll is the number of dolls divided by the total number of toys: 1/3.

questions.question: If you have 5 friends and you want to invite 3 of them to a party, how many ways can you do it?
questions.explanation: We can choose 3 friends out of 5 in C(5,3) ways, which is 10 ways.

questions.question: A bag contains 7 apples and 2 oranges. What is the probability of drawing an apple?
questions.explanation: The probability of drawing an apple is the number of apples divided by the total number of fruits: 7/9.

questions.question: A bookshelf contains 8 books, 3 of which are fiction. What is the probability of choosing a fiction book?
questions.explanation: The probability of choosing a fiction book is the number of fiction books divided by the total number of books: 3/8.

questions.question: If you have 4 siblings and you want to choose 2 of them to go to the park, how many ways can you do it?
questions.explanation: We can choose 2 siblings out of 4 in C(4,2) ways, which is 6 ways.

questions.question: A jar contains 15 marbles, 5 of which are red. What is the probability of drawing a red marble?
questions.explanation: The probability of drawing a red marble is the number of red marbles divided by the total number of marbles: 1/3.

title: 3 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-4-2-1 Analyzing data by using measures of central tendency and measures of dispersion, interpreting, and comparing between them.

questions.question: What is the range of a set of numbers?
questions.explanation: The range is the difference between the highest and lowest numbers in a set.

questions.question: What is an outlier in a set of numbers?
questions.explanation: An outlier is a number that is much higher or lower than the rest of the numbers in a set.

questions.question: What is the interquartile range (IQR)?
questions.explanation: The IQR is the difference between the third quartile and the first quartile.

questions.question: Which of the following is a measure of dispersion?
questions.explanation: Measures of dispersion include the range and interquartile range.

questions.question: What is the first quartile (Q1)?
questions.explanation: The first quartile is the middle value of the lower half of a set of numbers.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an outlier?
questions.explanation: An outlier is a number that is much higher or lower than the rest of the numbers in a set.

questions.question: What is the third quartile (Q3)?
questions.explanation: The third quartile is the middle value of the upper half of a set of numbers.

questions.question: What is the difference between the median and the mean?
questions.explanation: The difference between the median and the mean is a measure of dispersion.

questions.question: Which of the following is a type of measure of dispersion?
questions.explanation: Measures of dispersion include the range and interquartile range.

questions.question: What is the purpose of finding the range and interquartile range?
questions.explanation: The purpose of finding the range and interquartile range is to describe the spread of a set of numbers.

title: 4 - Math (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-4-4-2-1 Analyzing data by using measures of central tendency and measures of dispersion, interpreting, and comparing between them.

questions.question: What is the term for the average distance of each data point from the mean?
questions.explanation: Mean deviation is the average distance of each data point from the mean.

questions.question: Which measure of dispersion tells us how spread out the data points are from the mean?
questions.explanation: Standard deviation measures the spread of the data from the mean.

questions.question: What is the term for the sum of the squared differences between each data point and the mean?
questions.explanation: Variance is the sum of the squared differences between each data point and the mean, divided by the number of data points.

questions.question: What is the term for the square root of the variance?
questions.explanation: Standard deviation is the square root of the variance.

questions.question: What is the term for the difference between the largest and smallest data points?
questions.explanation: Range is the difference between the largest and smallest data points.

questions.question: Which measure of dispersion is used to describe the spread of the data?
questions.explanation: Standard deviation and variance are used to describe the spread of the data.

questions.question: What is the term for the average of the squared differences between each data point and the mean?
questions.explanation: Variance is the average of the squared differences between each data point and the mean.

questions.question: What is the term for the square root of the average of the squared differences between each data point and the mean?
questions.explanation: Standard deviation is the square root of the average of the squared differences between each data point and the mean.

questions.question: What is the term for the difference between the data points and the mean?
questions.explanation: Mean deviation is the difference between the data points and the mean.

questions.question: Which measure of dispersion is used to describe the spread of the data from the median?
questions.explanation: Mean deviation is used to describe the spread of the data from the median.

title: 4 - Math (Grade 1)

questions.question: There are 3 balls in a bag, 2 are red and 1 is blue. What is the probability of picking a red ball?
questions.explanation: The probability of picking a red ball is 2/3 because there are 2 red balls out of a total of 3 balls.

questions.question: A coin is flipped. What is the probability of getting heads?
questions.explanation: The probability of getting heads is 1/2 because there are 2 possible outcomes: heads or tails.

questions.question: There are 5 pencils in a jar, 3 are long and 2 are short. What is the probability of picking a short pencil?
questions.explanation: The probability of picking a short pencil is 2/5 because there are 2 short pencils out of a total of 5 pencils.

questions.question: A spinner has 8 equal sections. What is the probability of landing on a section with an even number?
questions.explanation: The probability of landing on a section with an even number is 1/2 because there are 4 even numbers out of a total of 8 numbers.

questions.question: There are 10 marbles in a bag, 5 are red and 5 are green. What is the probability of picking a green marble?
questions.explanation: The probability of picking a green marble is 1/2 because there are 5 green marbles out of a total of 10 marbles.

questions.question: A die is rolled. What is the probability of rolling a number greater than 4?
questions.explanation: The probability of rolling a number greater than 4 is 1/2 because there are 3 numbers greater than 4 out of a total of 6 numbers.

questions.question: There are 12 crayons in a box, 6 are blue and 6 are red. What is the probability of picking a blue crayon?
questions.explanation: The probability of picking a blue crayon is 1/2 because there are 6 blue crayons out of a total of 12 crayons.

questions.question: A bag contains 15 buttons, 5 are round and 10 are square. What is the probability of picking a round button?
questions.explanation: The probability of picking a round button is 1/3 because there are 5 round buttons out of a total of 15 buttons.

questions.question: A box contains 20 pencils, 10 are long and 10 are short. What is the probability of picking a short pencil?
questions.explanation: The probability of picking a short pencil is 1/2 because there are 10 short pencils out of a total of 20 pencils.

questions.question: A spinner has 12 equal sections. What is the probability of landing on a section with a multiple of 3?
questions.explanation: The probability of landing on a section with a multiple of 3 is 1/2 because there are 4 multiples of 3 out of a total of 12 numbers.

questions.question: What is the climate like on Mars?
questions.explanation: Mars is known to be a cold and dry planet.

questions.explanation: Saturn is a gas giant and its climate is cold and dry.

questions.explanation: Uranus is a gas giant and its climate is cold and dry.

questions.explanation: Neptune is a gas giant and its climate is cold and dry.

questions.explanation: Earth is known to have a hot and humid climate in some parts.

questions.explanation: Mercury is known to be a cold and dry planet.

title: 1 - Science (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-5-3-2-1 Explaining the causes of climate change, its effects and associated

questions.question: What is the main source of carbon in the Earth's atmosphere?
questions.explanation: Animals and humans release carbon into the atmosphere through breathing and burning fossil fuels.

questions.question: What is the process by which plants take in carbon dioxide?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants use sunlight to convert carbon dioxide and water into glucose and oxygen.

questions.question: Where is most of the Earth's carbon stored?
questions.explanation: Most of the Earth's carbon is stored in rocks and soil, with a smaller amount in the atmosphere and living organisms.

questions.question: What happens to carbon in the atmosphere when it is released?
options: It stays in the atmosphere forever
It is absorbed by the oceans
questions.explanation: Carbon in the atmosphere is eventually absorbed by the oceans, where it can remain for thousands of years.

questions.question: How do humans release carbon into the atmosphere?
options: By breathing and burning fossil fuels
By planting trees and growing crops
questions.explanation: Humans release carbon into the atmosphere through breathing and burning fossil fuels, such as gasoline and coal.

questions.question: What is the role of the oceans in the Earth's carbon cycle?
options: To release carbon into the atmosphere
To transport carbon between the atmosphere and land
To have no impact on the carbon cycle
questions.explanation: The oceans play a crucial role in the Earth's carbon cycle by absorbing and storing carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.

questions.question: How is carbon transported between the atmosphere and land?
questions.explanation: Carbon is transported between the atmosphere and land through the carbon cycle, which includes processes such as photosynthesis and respiration.

questions.question: What is the impact of increased carbon dioxide in the atmosphere?
options: It has no impact on the planet
It causes weather patterns to change
questions.explanation: Increased carbon dioxide in the atmosphere traps heat and contributes to global warming.

questions.question: How can we reduce our carbon footprint?
options: By reducing energy consumption and using renewable energy sources
By increasing air travel and transportation
questions.explanation: We can reduce our carbon footprint by reducing energy consumption and using renewable energy sources, such as solar and wind power.

questions.question: What is the importance of carbon in the Earth's ecosystem?
options: It has no impact on the ecosystem
It is necessary for plant growth
It is necessary for animal life
It is necessary for the Earth's climate
questions.explanation: Carbon is essential for life on Earth, as it is a key component of living organisms and plays a critical role in the Earth's climate.

questions.question: What happens to the plants and animals after they die?
options: C. They become part of the soil.
D. They are eaten by other animals.
questions.explanation: After plants and animals die, their bodies become part of the soil and help plants grow.

questions.question: Why is organic carbon important?
options: A. Because it is used to make toys.
B. Because it helps plants grow.
C. Because it makes animals sick.
D. Because it is used to make rocks.
questions.explanation: Organic carbon is important because it helps plants grow and makes food for animals.

questions.question: What do plants and animals use to grow?
questions.explanation: Plants and animals use organic carbon and water to grow.

questions.question: What happens to the carbon in plants after they die?
options: C. It becomes part of the soil.
D. It is eaten by other animals.
questions.explanation: After plants die, the carbon in their bodies becomes part of the soil.

questions.question: Why is the carbon cycle important?
options: A. Because it makes animals sick.
B. Because it helps plants grow.
D. Because it keeps the Earth healthy.
questions.explanation: The carbon cycle is important because it helps keep the Earth healthy by making food for plants and animals.

questions.question: What do plants and animals turn into after they die?
questions.explanation: After plants and animals die, they turn into soil.

questions.question: What is organic carbon used for?
questions.explanation: Organic carbon is used to help plants grow.

questions.question: Why is the soil important?
options: A. Because it is used to make toys.
B. Because it helps plants grow.
C. Because it makes animals sick.
D. Because it is used to make rocks.
questions.explanation: The soil is important because it helps plants grow.

questions.question: What happens to the carbon in animals after they die?
options: C. It becomes part of the soil.
D. It is eaten by other animals.
questions.explanation: After animals die, the carbon in their bodies becomes part of the soil.

questions.question: What is the result of the carbon cycle?
questions.explanation: The result of the carbon cycle is keeping the Earth healthy by making food for plants and animals.

title: 1 - Science (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-5-3-2-3 Describing the natural cycles and identifying their causes and benefits.

questions.question: What is the reason for the changing seasons?
options: A. The Earth is moving closer to the Sun
B. The Earth rotates on its side
C. The Sun is moving farther from the Earth
questions.explanation: The Earth's tilt on its axis is the main reason for the changing seasons.

questions.question: What is the process called when plants make their own food?
questions.explanation: Photosynthesis is the process by which plants use sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide to produce glucose and oxygen.

questions.question: Why do we experience day and night?
options: A. The Earth is moving closer to or farther from the Sun
B. The Earth rotates on its axis
C. The Sun is shining brightly all the time
D. The Earth's atmosphere blocks the Sun's light
questions.explanation: The Earth's rotation on its axis is the main reason for day and night.

questions.question: What is the water cycle?
options: A. The process of water flowing underground
B. The process of water evaporating, condensing, and precipitating
C. The process of plants making their own food
D. The process of animals drinking water
questions.explanation: The water cycle is the continuous process by which water is circulated between the Earth's oceans, atmosphere, and land.

questions.question: Why do we need the water cycle?
options: A. To keep the Earth cool
B. To provide water for plants and animals
questions.explanation: The water cycle is essential for providing water for plants and animals to survive.

questions.question: What is the process called when animals and plants die and decompose?
questions.explanation: Decomposition is the process by which animals and plants break down into simpler substances.

questions.question: Why do we need decomposition?
options: A. To keep the Earth clean
B. To provide nutrients for plants
C. To create new living things
questions.explanation: Decomposition is essential for providing nutrients for plants to grow.

questions.question: What is the process called when plants release water vapor into the air?
questions.explanation: Evaporation is the process by which plants release water vapor into the air.

questions.question: Why do we need evaporation?
options: A. To keep the Earth cool
B. To provide water for plants and animals
questions.explanation: Evaporation is essential for providing water for plants and animals to survive.

questions.question: What is the process called when water vapor in the air condenses into droplets?
questions.explanation: Condensation is the process by which water vapor in the air condenses into droplets.

title: 2 - Science (Grade 1)
outcomeText: 9-5-3-2-3 Describing the natural cycles and identifying their causes and benefits.

questions.question: What happens to the day and night when we move from winter to summer?
options: The day gets shorter and the night gets longer
The day gets longer and the night gets shorter
The day stays the same and the night stays the same
The day and night stay the same
questions.explanation: In the summer, the Earth is tilted towards the Sun, resulting in longer days and shorter nights.

questions.question: Why do we have different seasons?
options: Because of the Sun's movement around the Earth
Because of the Earth's movement around the Sun
Because of the wind and rain
Because of the mountains and valleys
questions.explanation: The Earth's tilt and orbit around the Sun cause the changing seasons.

questions.question: What is the main reason for the changes in the weather?
options: The movement of the Sun and the Earth
questions.explanation: The movement of the Sun and the Earth affects the weather patterns on our planet.

questions.question: Why do we have rainbows after it rains?
options: Because of the Sun's rays passing through the clouds
Because of the wind blowing through the clouds
Because of the rain falling from the clouds
Because of the mountains blocking the Sun's rays
questions.explanation: Rainbows are formed when the Sun's rays pass through water droplets in the air, creating a spectrum of colors.

questions.question: What is the main cause of the tides?
options: The movement of the Earth's core
The movement of the Moon and the Sun
The movement of the wind and the rain
questions.explanation: The gravitational pull of the Moon and the Sun causes the tides to rise and fall.

questions.question: Why do we have different types of clouds?
options: Because of the movement of the wind
Because of the movement of the Sun
Because of the movement of the Earth's core
Because of the temperature and humidity in the air
questions.explanation: Clouds are formed when water vapor in the air condenses, and their types depend on the temperature and humidity in the air.

questions.question: What is the main reason for the formation of rivers?
options: Because of the movement of the wind
Because of the movement of the rain
Because of the movement of the Earth's core
Because of the movement of the mountains and the water flowing downhill
questions.explanation: Rivers are formed when water flows downhill from the mountains and collects in low-lying areas.

questions.question: Why do we have different types of weather?
options: Because of the movement of the wind
Because of the movement of the Sun
Because of the movement of the Earth's core
Because of the temperature and humidity in the air
questions.explanation: Weather is affected by various factors, including the movement of the Sun, the temperature and humidity in the air, and the movement of the wind.

questions.question: What is the main cause of the changes in the ocean's level?
options: The movement of the Earth's core
The movement of the Moon and the Sun
The movement of the wind and the rain
questions.explanation: The gravitational pull of the Moon and the Sun causes the ocean's level to rise and fall.

questions.question: Why do we have different types of landforms?
options: Because of the movement of the wind
Because of the movement of the rain
Because of the movement of the Earth's core
Because of the movement of the mountains and the erosion
questions.explanation: Landforms are shaped by geological processes, including erosion, weathering, and plate tectonics.
